presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The documents reflect distinct control mechanisms—one focused on evidence completeness for vendor control closure, the other on ongoing governance of privileged access with strict lifecycle requirements. While the first emphasizes proactive verification and resolution of missing artifacts to close controls, the second underscores continuous oversight, recertification, and accountability in privileged account management.

- Evidence gaps must be resolved before control closure, requiring documented provenance.  
- Missing artifacts can be validated via archived email-export evidence (EV-002).  
- Privileged access requires quarterly recertification and logging integration.  
- Access approvals are contingent on strict scoping and logging controls.  
- Personnel changes trigger immediate revalidation of access rights.  
- Governance models differ: one is evidence-driven for control closure; the other is lifecycle-driven for access accountability.
key_highlights: The documents reflect distinct control mechanisms—one focused on evidence completeness for vendor control closure, the other on ongoing governance of privileged access with strict lifecycle requirements. While the first emphasizes proactive verification and resolution of missing artifacts to close controls, the second underscores continuous oversight, recertification, and accountability in privileged account management.
Evidence gaps must be resolved before control closure, requiring documented provenance.
Missing artifacts can be validated via archived email-export evidence (EV-002).
Privileged access requires quarterly recertification and logging integration.
Access approvals are contingent on strict scoping and logging controls.
Personnel changes trigger immediate revalidation of access rights.
